steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank fills in a noun phrase where Mark A. Lee identifies which researcher led the study. This is a restrictive appositive — it narrows down exactly who, so no commas around it. Periods after initials must also be present.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — researcher, Mark A Lee,: Missing period after the middle initial. ✗
  • Choice B — researcher Mark A. Lee,: Inserts a comma that separates the appositive from the rest of the sentence incorrectly. ✗
  • Choice C — researcher Mark A. Lee: Restrictive appositive with no commas and correct punctuation on the initial. ✓
  • Choice D — researcher, Mark A. Lee: Uses an unnecessary comma after "researcher" before the restrictive appositive. ✗
That matches Choice C — researcher Mark A. Lee.
